title: revert: remove legacy app Worker route cleanup
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Reverts PR #11783.
- Removes the legacy Worker route deletion helper from app cutover.
- Keeps the Websim registry, Worker deploy target, token fix, and public routing changes intact.

Checks:
- npx biome check --write apps/_scripts/deploy-app.js
- git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD
- curl https://websim.pollinations.ai/api/health

gist.headline: Undo: Legacy Route Cleanup
gist.blurb: Rolled back a recent change to our app deployment scripts, keeping legacy Worker routes intact for now while preserving our new routing upgrades.
gist.summary: Reverted the addition of the legacy Worker route deletion helper in the app deployment script (`deploy-app.js`).
gist.impact: Internal deployment processes will no longer automatically delete legacy Worker routes during app cutovers, though recent Websim registry and public routing changes remain active.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot (Polly) hitting a glowing 'undo' button or putting a piece of code back into a server rack, assisted by the monitor-head robot.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot pressing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) 'UNDO' button on a retro terminal. A chunky monitor-head robot happily holds a restored floppy disk. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
